result:
Done — `python3 -m pytest -q` now passes all 6 tests. There were two bugs in `lrucache/cache.py`:

1. **`get` didn't refresh recency** (`cache.py:30`). A hit returned the value without moving the key to the most-recently-used end of the `OrderedDict`, so reading an entry didn't protect it from eviction — the next `put` evicted it anyway. Fixed by adding `move_to_end(key)` on a non-expired hit.

2. **`put` evicted even when updating an existing key** (`cache.py:33`). The eviction check only looked at `len >= capacity`, so overwriting a key in a full cache wrongly kicked out the least-recently-used entry. Fixed by evicting only when the key is new and the cache is at capacity.

`SOLUTION.md` is written at the repo root with the same explanation, and nothing under `tests/` was touched.
